political.s: FEC: $171K in executive political donations; partisan lean Democratic +22 across 7 executive donors.

charity.s: $6M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.11% of revenue) via AEO Foundation.

environment.s: Via parent company American Airlines: CDP Climate Change: B (2024) — management-band disclosure; discloses emissions data but limited target ambition.

dei.s: Via parent company American Airlines: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

privacy.s: Via parent company American Airlines: DOT enforcement: Record $50M DOT civil penalty — failure to provide timely refunds and mistreatment of wheelchair-using passengers; largest single-carrier enforcement action in agency history.

execPay.s: Via parent company American Airlines: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 155:1 (median employee pay $89,429) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.extract: American Eagle is the brand for regional airline flights operated for American Airlines, encompassing flights by wholly owned affiliates Envoy Air, PSA Airlines, and Piedmont Airlines, as well as third-party carriers Republic Airways and SkyWest Airlines. These regional carriers serve smaller markets, facilitating connections to American Airlines hubs, and supporting operations in mainline markets. All American Eagle carriers share similar logos, uniforms, and aircraft paint schemes as American Airlines’ mainline operations. In 2023, 46 million passengers flew on American Eagle regional flight

sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves toddler girl pants, jeans and shorts sold in various styles. The style number is printed on a sewn-in label located under the care/content label on the inside of the waistband. The garments were sold in sizes 12-18 months through 5 years. sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level  unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: At 10:30 a.m. on June 12, 2025, an employee was working from a 10-foot ladder, replacing an LED driver in an office lighting fixture. He slipped on rain water and fell off the ladder, landed on his feet, and then fell to his buttocks. He suffered a lower back injury that resulted in hospitalization and surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Contact with hot objects or substances
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was removing dumplings from a chaffing dish when a lit fuel can spilled onto the employee, resulting in a partial thickness burn to their lower left leg.

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught in running equipment or machinery, n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: Two employees were loading paper onto a conveyor belt system. One of them was on the conveyor when it went into reverse. The employee's right foot was pulled into a metal floor plate and injured. The incident took place during a training activity.

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught in running equipment or machinery during regular operation
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was threading the core on a paper winder. A nip point in the winder pinched the tip of his left middle finger, removing the skin at the tip. The bone tip was medically amputated.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to tripping over an object
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee tripped over an employee's feet and struck a locker door, injuring her head.
